title: W2 A Convex Sets

Mathematical Economics

     Juan Carlos Carbajal

  Week 2A    —    Term 1, 2026




                                    1/11
 Convex Sets




              2/11
                        Convex Combinations



Given x, y ∈ R and 0 ≤ α ≤ 1, a convex combination of x and y is the real
number
                            α x + (1 − α) y.

                        Convex Combinations


Given x, y ∈ R and 0 ≤ α ≤ 1, a convex combination of x and y is the real
number
                            α x + (1 − α) y.

                                                                            3/11
                        Convex Combinations


Given x, y ∈ R and 0 ≤ α ≤ 1, a convex combination of x and y is the real
number
                            α x + (1 − α) y.



                  x      αx + (1 − α)y              y




                                                                            3/11
                         Convex Combinations


Given x, y ∈ R and 0 ≤ α ≤ 1, a convex combination of x and y is the real
number
                             α x + (1 − α) y.



                   x      αx + (1 − α)y               y



If α ∈ (0, 1) then z = αx + (1 − α)y is a number strictly between x and y.

 Exercise 1

   Fix x < y in R. Show that for any z ∈ R satisfying x < z < y, there
   is some α ∈ (0, 1) such that z is a convex combination of x and y.




                                                                             3/11
                   Convex Combinations in Rn


Definition 1

 Given x, y ∈ Rn , a convex combination of x and y is any vector
 of the form
               α x + (1 − α) y,     for any 0 ≤ α ≤ 1.


                    x2
                             α=0
                         y     α = 0.3


                                    α = 0.7

                                         α=1
                                   x
                                               x1




                                                                   4/11
                         Convex Sets in Rn



Definition 2

 A set C ⊆ Rn is convex if for all x, y ∈ C and all 0 ≤ α ≤ 1, one has
 that
                        α x + (1 − α) y ∈ C.


               x2                                 x2




                              x1                                   x1




                                                                         5/11
                        Convex Sets in Rn


Example 1

 The set S = {x ∈ R : |x| ≤ 1} is a convex subset of R.
 But the set T = {x ∈ R : |x| ≥ 1} is not.




                                                          6/11
                          Convex Sets in Rn


Example 1

 The set S = {x ∈ R : |x| ≤ 1} is a convex subset of R.
 But the set T = {x ∈ R : |x| ≥ 1} is not.



Example 2

 In an economy with n goods, the competitive budget set
                   B(p, w) = {x ∈ Rn : p · x ≤ w}
 is also a convex set.




                                                          6/11
                     Hyperplanes and Half-Spaces

Any interval in R is a convex set.

Any line L = {(x1 , x2 ) ∈ R2 : a1 x1 + a2 x2 = β} is also convex.
                                     x2




                                                            x1




                                                                     7/11
                    Hyperplanes and Half-Spaces


 Definition 3

   Given a ∈ Rn , a ̸= 0, and β ∈ R, a hyperplane H in Rn is

                      H = {x ∈ Rn : a · x = β}.

   The half-spaces H + and H − are given by

   H + = {x ∈ Rn : a · x ≥ β}     and   H − = {x ∈ Rn : a · x ≤ β}.



One can show that hyperplanes and half-spaces are convex sets.

 Example 3

   The budget line L(p, w) = {x ∈ Rn : p · x = w} is a hyperplane.




                                                                      8/11
 Operations with Convex Sets




                              9/11
                        Union of Convex Sets



The union of two convex sets may be convex.


               x2                             x2



                               x1                  x1




                                                        10/11
                  Intersection of Convex Sets




Proposition 1

 Let B and C be two convex sets in Rn . The set A = B ∩ C is convex.




                                                                       11/11
                     Intersection of Convex Sets




 Proposition 1

   Let B and C be two convex sets in Rn . The set A = B ∩ C is convex.


Proof   Suppose B and C are convex subsets of Rn . Let x, y be two
arbitrary elements of B ∩ C and let 0 ≤ α ≤ 1 be given.




                                                                         11/11
                     Intersection of Convex Sets




 Proposition 1

   Let B and C be two convex sets in Rn . The set A = B ∩ C is convex.


Proof   Suppose B and C are convex subsets of Rn . Let x, y be two
arbitrary elements of B ∩ C and let 0 ≤ α ≤ 1 be given.
  ◦ Since x and y belong to B, it follows that αx + (1 − α)y ∈ B.

  ◦ A similar statement holds for C, thus αx + (1 − α)y ∈ C.
It follows that αx + (1 − α)y ∈ A = B ∩ C, as desired.
